ЁЭФ╝xтИ╝ЁЭТ│тБв[тИСi=1ksi(1)тБв(x)тБвc^iтБв(x)]тй╛ЁЭФ╝xтИ╝ЁЭТ│тБв[тИСi=1ksi(2)тБв(x)тБвc^iтБв(x)]subscriptЁЭФ╝similar-toЁЭСеЁЭТ│delimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsubscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа1ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭФ╝similar-toЁЭСеЁЭТ│delimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsubscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа2ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе\mathbb{E}_{x\sim\mathcal{X}}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s^{(1)}_{i}(x)\hat{c}_{i}(x)% \right]\geqslant\mathbb{E}_{x\sim\mathcal{X}}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s^{(2)}_{i}(x% )\hat{c}_{i}(x)\right]blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИ╝ caligraphic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ] тй╛ blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИ╝ caligraphic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ]
Proof.

We show that for any xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X, the cost of s(1)superscriptЁЭСа1s^{(1)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is greater or equal to the cost of s(2)superscriptЁЭСа2s^{(2)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Let xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X be arbitrary. Suppose s(1)superscriptЁЭСа1s^{(1)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is strictly cheaper than s(2)superscriptЁЭСа2s^{(2)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Then, there must exist a model pair i,jЁЭСЦЁЭСЧi,jitalic_i , italic_j such that c^iтБв(x)<c^jтБв(x)subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{c}_{i}(x)<\hat{c}_{j}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) < over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ), si(1)тБв(x)>si(2)тБв(x)тй╛0subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа1ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа2ЁЭСЦЁЭСе0s^{(1)}_{i}(x)>s^{(2)}_{i}(x)\geqslant 0italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) тй╛ 0, and sj(2)тБв(x)>sj(1)тБв(x)тй╛0subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа2ЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа1ЁЭСЧЁЭСе0s^{(2)}_{j}(x)>s^{(1)}_{j}(x)\geqslant 0italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) тй╛ 0. However, si(1)тБв(x)>0subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа1ЁЭСЦЁЭСе0s^{(1)}_{i}(x)>0italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > 0 implies

q^iтБв(x)тИТ╬╗1тБвc^iтБв(x)тй╛q^jтБв(x)тИТ╬╗1тБвc^jтБв(x).subscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q}_{i}(x)-\lambda_{1}\hat{c}_{i}(x)\geqslant\hat{q}_{j}(x)-\lambda_{1}% \hat{c}_{j}(x).over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) тй╛ over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) .

Furthermore, since ╬╗1тИТ╬╗2<0subscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ20\lambda_{1}-\lambda_{2}<0italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < 0, we have

c^iтБв(x)тБв(╬╗1тИТ╬╗2)>c^jтБв(x)тБв(╬╗1тИТ╬╗2).subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\hat{c}_{i}(x)(\lambda_{1}-\lambda_{2})>\hat{c}_{j}(x)(\lambda_{1}-\lambda_{2}).over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) > over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) .

Adding these two inequalities gives

q^iтБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвc^iтБв(x)>q^jтБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвc^jтБв(x),subscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2subscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q}_{i}(x)-\lambda_{2}\hat{c}_{i}(x)>\hat{q}_{j}(x)-\lambda_{2}\hat{c}_{j}% (x),over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ,

which is a contradiction with sj(2)тБв(x)>0subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСа2ЁЭСЧЁЭСе0s^{(2)}_{j}(x)>0italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > 0. Thus, the cost of s(1)superscriptЁЭСа1s^{(1)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is greater or equal to the cost of s(2)superscriptЁЭСа2s^{(2)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. тИО

Lemma 4.

Let ╬Ы╬Ы\Lambdaroman_╬Ы be the set of points ╬╗тИИтДЭЁЭЬЖтДЭ\lambda\in\mathbb{R}italic_╬╗ тИИ blackboard_R such that there exist an xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X and iтЙаjЁЭСЦЁЭСЧi\neq jitalic_i тЙа italic_j such that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвci^тБв(x)=qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{i}}(x)=\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) = over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). Let ╬╗1<╬╗2subscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda_{1}<\lambda_{2}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be such that [╬╗1,╬╗2]тИй╬Ы=тИЕsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2╬Ы[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]\cap\Lambda=\emptyset[ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] тИй roman_╬Ы = тИЕ. Then, S╬╗1=S╬╗2subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2S_{\lambda_{1}}=S_{\lambda_{2}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Furthermore, if [╬╗1,╬╗2]тИй╬Ы={╬╗тИЧ}subscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2╬ЫsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]\cap\Lambda=\{\lambda^{*}\}[ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] тИй roman_╬Ы = { italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT }, then S╬╗тКВS╬╗тИЧsubscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda}\subset S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тКВ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for all ╬╗тИИ[╬╗1,╬╗2]ЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]italic_╬╗ тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ].

Proof.

We first show the first statement by showing that S╬╗1тИЦS╬╗2=тИЕsubscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2S_{\lambda_{1}}\setminus S_{\lambda_{2}}=\emptysetitalic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИЦ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = тИЕ. S╬╗2тИЦS╬╗1=тИЕsubscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1S_{\lambda_{2}}\setminus S_{\lambda_{1}}=\emptysetitalic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИЦ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = тИЕ follows analogously. Suppose there exists a routing strategy sтИИS╬╗1тИЦS╬╗2ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2s\in S_{\lambda_{1}}\setminus S_{\lambda_{2}}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИЦ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since sтИЙS╬╗2ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2s\notin S_{\lambda_{2}}italic_s тИЙ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, there must exist an xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X and model iЁЭСЦiitalic_i such that siтБв(x)>0subscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦЁЭСе0s_{i}(x)>0italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > 0 and qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвci^тБв(x)<maxjтБбqj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭСЧ^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda_{2}\hat{c_{i}}(x)<\max_{j}\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda_{2}% \hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) < roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). Let jЁЭСЧjitalic_j be an index such that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвci^тБв(x)<qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗2тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda_{2}\hat{c_{i}}(x)<\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda_{2}\hat{c_{j}}% (x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) < over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). Since sтИИS╬╗1ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1s\in S_{\lambda_{1}}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, we have qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗1тБвci^тБв(x)тй╛qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗1тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda_{1}\hat{c_{i}}(x)\geqslant\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda_{1}% \hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) тй╛ over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). By continuity, there exists a ╬╗тИИ[╬╗1,╬╗2]ЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]italic_╬╗ тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] such that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвci^тБв(x)=qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{i}}(x)=\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) = over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ), which is a contradiction with [╬╗1,╬╗2]тИй╬Ы=тИЕsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2╬Ы[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]\cap\Lambda=\emptyset[ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] тИй roman_╬Ы = тИЕ.

Now suppose [╬╗1,╬╗2]тИй╬Ы={╬╗тИЧ}subscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2╬ЫsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]\cap\Lambda=\{\lambda^{*}\}[ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] тИй roman_╬Ы = { italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT }. Let ╬╗тИИ[╬╗1,╬╗тИЧ)ЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1superscriptЁЭЬЖ\lambda\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda^{*})italic_╬╗ тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) be arbitrary and let sтИИS╬╗ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖs\in S_{\lambda}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be arbitrary. We show that sтИИS╬╗тИЧЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖs\in S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. For ╬╗тИИ(╬╗тИЧ,╬╗2]ЁЭЬЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda\in(\lambda^{*},\lambda_{2}]italic_╬╗ тИИ ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ], the proof is completely analogous. By contradiction, suppose there exists an xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X and model iЁЭСЦiitalic_i such that siтБв(x)>0subscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦЁЭСе0s_{i}(x)>0italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) > 0 and qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тИЧтБвci^тБв(x)<maxjтБбqj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тИЧтБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭСЧ^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda^{*}\hat{c_{i}}(x)<\max_{j}\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda^{*}% \hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) < roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). This means there exists a model jЁЭСЧjitalic_j such that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тИЧтБвci^тБв(x)<qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тИЧтБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda^{*}\hat{c_{i}}(x)<\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda^{*}\hat{c_{j}}% (x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) < over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). Since sтИИS╬╗ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖs\in S_{\lambda}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, we know that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвci^тБв(x)тй╛qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{i}}(x)\geqslant\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda\hat{c_{j}}% (x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) тй╛ over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). This implies that there must exist a ╬╗тА▓тИИ[╬╗1,╬╗тИЧ)superscriptЁЭЬЖтА▓subscriptЁЭЬЖ1superscriptЁЭЬЖ\lambda^{\prime}\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda^{*})italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) such that qi^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тА▓тБвci^тБв(x)=qj^тБв(x)тИТ╬╗тА▓тБвcj^тБв(x)^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖтА▓^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСе^subscriptЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСеsuperscriptЁЭЬЖтА▓^subscriptЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q_{i}}(x)-\lambda^{\prime}\hat{c_{i}}(x)=\hat{q_{j}}(x)-\lambda^{\prime}% \hat{c_{j}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) = over^ start_ARG italic_q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ( italic_x ). However, this is a contradiction with [╬╗1,╬╗тИЧ)тИй╬Ы=тИЕsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1superscriptЁЭЬЖ╬Ы[\lambda_{1},\lambda^{*})\cap\Lambda=\emptyset[ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) тИй roman_╬Ы = тИЕ. Thus, sтИИS╬╗тИЧЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖs\in S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. тИО

In what follows, we will assume that |╬Ы|<тИЮ╬Ы|\Lambda|<\infty| roman_╬Ы | < тИЮ. This is a very minor assumption. For instance, if q^^ЁЭСЮ\hat{q}over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG and c^^ЁЭСР\hat{c}over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG only take on a finite amount of values, this is trivially satisfied. Since estimators are implemented on a computer, they will always have a finite precision, meaning that q^^ЁЭСЮ\hat{q}over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG and c^^ЁЭСР\hat{c}over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG will only take on a finite amount of values.

Lemma 5.

Let ╬╗1<╬╗2subscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda_{1}<\lambda_{2}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and s(1)superscriptЁЭСа1s^{(1)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, resp. s(2)superscriptЁЭСа2s^{(2)}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT be arbitrary routing strategies in S╬╗1subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1S_{\lambda_{1}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, resp. S╬╗2subscriptЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ2S_{\lambda_{2}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, with costs resp. B1subscriptЁЭР╡1B_{1}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and B2subscriptЁЭР╡2B_{2}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Then, for any BтИИ[B1,B2]ЁЭР╡subscriptЁЭР╡1subscriptЁЭР╡2B\in[B_{1},B_{2}]italic_B тИИ [ italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] there exists a ╬╗тИИ[╬╗1,╬╗2]ЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]italic_╬╗ тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] such that S╬╗subscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contains a routing strategy that has exactly cost BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B.

Proof.

Let BтИИ[B1,B2]ЁЭР╡subscriptЁЭР╡1subscriptЁЭР╡2B\in[B_{1},B_{2}]italic_B тИИ [ italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] be arbitrary. If B=B1ЁЭР╡subscriptЁЭР╡1B=B_{1}italic_B = italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT or B=B2ЁЭР╡subscriptЁЭР╡2B=B_{2}italic_B = italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, the statement is trivially true. Therefore, suppose BтИИ(B1,B2)ЁЭР╡subscriptЁЭР╡1subscriptЁЭР╡2B\in(B_{1},B_{2})italic_B тИИ ( italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ). Let ╬Ы╬Ы\Lambdaroman_╬Ы be as defined in Lemma┬а4. By Lemma┬а3, there exists a ╬╗тИЧтИИ[╬╗1,╬╗2]superscriptЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1subscriptЁЭЬЖ2\lambda^{*}\in[\lambda_{1},\lambda_{2}]italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ [ italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ] such that all strategies in S╬╗subscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for ╬╗<╬╗тИЧЁЭЬЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ\lambda<\lambda^{*}italic_╬╗ < italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, resp. ╬╗>╬╗тИЧЁЭЬЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ\lambda>\lambda^{*}italic_╬╗ > italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, have cost at least, resp. at most, BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. If ╬╗тИЧтИЙ╬ЫsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ╬Ы\lambda^{*}\notin\Lambdaitalic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЙ roman_╬Ы, then the first part of Lemma┬а4, together with |╬Ы|<тИЮ╬Ы|\Lambda|<\infty| roman_╬Ы | < тИЮ, implies that S╬╗тИЧ=S╬╗тИЧтИТ╧╡=S╬╗тИЧ+╧╡subscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡subscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡S_{\lambda^{*}}=S_{\lambda^{*}-\epsilon}=S_{\lambda^{*}+\epsilon}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for some ╧╡>0italic-╧╡0\epsilon>0italic_╧╡ > 0. All the strategies in S╬╗тИЧsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda^{*}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT must therefore have cost both at least and at most BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B, meaning they should equal BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. We can therefore assume that ╬╗тИЧтИИ╬ЫsuperscriptЁЭЬЖ╬Ы\lambda^{*}\in\Lambdaitalic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ roman_╬Ы. By Lemma┬а4 and |╬Ы|<тИЮ╬Ы|\Lambda|<\infty| roman_╬Ы | < тИЮ, there is en ╧╡>0italic-╧╡0\epsilon>0italic_╧╡ > 0 such that S╬╗тИЧтИТ╧╡тКВS╬╗тИЧsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡subscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda^{*}-\epsilon}\subset S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тКВ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and S╬╗тИЧ+╧╡тКВS╬╗тИЧsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡subscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda^{*}+\epsilon}\subset S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тКВ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Let sтИТтИИS╬╗тИЧтИТ╧╡superscriptЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡s^{-}\in S_{\lambda^{*}-\epsilon}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and s+тИИS╬╗тИЧ+╧╡superscriptЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖitalic-╧╡s^{+}\in S_{\lambda^{*}+\epsilon}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_╧╡ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be arbitrary. Let s╬│superscriptЁЭСаЁЭЫ╛s^{\gamma}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_╬│ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT be the convex combination of sтИТsuperscriptЁЭСаs^{-}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and s+superscriptЁЭСаs^{+}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT with weight ╬│тИИ[0,1]ЁЭЫ╛01\gamma\in[0,1]italic_╬│ тИИ [ 0 , 1 ]. Since sтИТ,s+тИИS╬╗тИЧsuperscriptЁЭСаsuperscriptЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖs^{-},s^{+}\in S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT , italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, we have s╬│тИИS╬╗тИЧsuperscriptЁЭСаЁЭЫ╛subscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖs^{\gamma}\in S_{\lambda^{*}}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_╬│ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT by Lemma┬а2. Denote by BтИТsuperscriptЁЭР╡B^{-}italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, resp. B+superscriptЁЭР╡B^{+}italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, the cost of sтИТsuperscriptЁЭСаs^{-}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, resp s+superscriptЁЭСаs^{+}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Furthermore, the cost of s╬│superscriptЁЭСаЁЭЫ╛s^{\gamma}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_╬│ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is ╬│тБвBтИТ+(1тИТ╬│)тБвB+ЁЭЫ╛superscriptЁЭР╡1ЁЭЫ╛superscriptЁЭР╡\gamma B^{-}+(1-\gamma)B^{+}italic_╬│ italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + ( 1 - italic_╬│ ) italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Since BтИИ[BтИТ,B+]ЁЭР╡superscriptЁЭР╡superscriptЁЭР╡B\in[B^{-},B^{+}]italic_B тИИ [ italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT , italic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], there exists a ╬│тИИ[0,1]ЁЭЫ╛01\gamma\in[0,1]italic_╬│ тИИ [ 0 , 1 ] such that s╬│superscriptЁЭСаЁЭЫ╛s^{\gamma}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_╬│ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT has cost exactly BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. тИО

We can now prove the theorem.

Proof.

If S0subscriptЁЭСЖ0S_{0}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contains a solution that has cost less than or equal to BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B, then this solution trivially achieves the optimal quality. Thus, for the rest of the proof we can assume that the cost of every solution in S0subscriptЁЭСЖ0S_{0}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is greater than BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. For ╬╗тЖТтИЮтЖТЁЭЬЖ\lambda\rightarrow\inftyitalic_╬╗ тЖТ тИЮ, S╬╗subscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contains the solution that assigns all probability mass to the model with the lowest cost. Since there is an admissible solution, this solution necessarily has cost less than BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. Therefore, by Lemma┬а5, there exists a ╬╗тИЧтИИтДЭsuperscriptЁЭЬЖтДЭ\lambda^{*}\in\mathbb{R}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ blackboard_R such that S╬╗тИЧsubscriptЁЭСЖsuperscriptЁЭЬЖS_{\lambda^{*}}italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЧ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contains a routing strategy that has exactly cost BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B.

Let sЁЭСаsitalic_s be an arbitrary routing strategy in тЛГ╬╗тИИтДЭ+S╬╗subscriptЁЭЬЖsuperscriptтДЭsubscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖ\bigcup_{\lambda\in\mathbb{R}^{+}}S_{\lambda}тЛГ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ тИИ blackboard_R start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT that has cost BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B. Specifically, let sтИИS╬╗ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭСЖЁЭЬЖs\in S_{\lambda}italic_s тИИ italic_S start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Let sтА▓superscriptЁЭСатА▓s^{\prime}italic_s start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT be any other routing strategy that is an admissible solution to the optimization problem. Then:

ЁЭФ╝xтИИXтБв[тИСi=1ksiтА▓тБв(x)тБвq^iтБв(x)]subscriptЁЭФ╝ЁЭСеЁЭСЛdelimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦтА▓ЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСе\displaystyle\mathbb{E}_{x\in X}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s_{i}^{\prime}(x)\hat{q}_{% i}(x)\right]blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИИ italic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ] =ЁЭФ╝xтИИXтБв[тИСi=1ksiтА▓тБв(x)тБвq^iтБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвB+╬╗тБвB]absentsubscriptЁЭФ╝ЁЭСеЁЭСЛdelimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦтА▓ЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖЁЭР╡ЁЭЬЖЁЭР╡\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}_{x\in X}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s_{i}^{\prime}(x)\hat{q}_% {i}(x)-\lambda B+\lambda B\right]= blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИИ italic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ italic_B + italic_╬╗ italic_B ]
тй╜ЁЭФ╝xтИИXтБв[тИСi=1ksiтА▓тБв(x)тБв(q^iтБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвc^iтБв(x))+╬╗тБвB]absentsubscriptЁЭФ╝ЁЭСеЁЭСЛdelimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦтА▓ЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖЁЭР╡\displaystyle\leqslant\mathbb{E}_{x\in X}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s_{i}^{\prime}(x)% \left(\hat{q}_{i}(x)-\lambda\hat{c}_{i}(x)\right)+\lambda B\right]тй╜ blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИИ italic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ( over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ) + italic_╬╗ italic_B ]
тй╜ЁЭФ╝xтИИXтБв[тИСi=1ksiтБв(x)тБв(q^iтБв(x)тИТ╬╗тБвc^iтБв(x))+╬╗тБвB]absentsubscriptЁЭФ╝ЁЭСеЁЭСЛdelimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsubscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦЁЭСеЁЭЬЖЁЭР╡\displaystyle\leqslant\mathbb{E}_{x\in X}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s_{i}(x)\left(% \hat{q}_{i}(x)-\lambda\hat{c}_{i}(x)\right)+\lambda B\right]тй╜ blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИИ italic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ( over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ) + italic_╬╗ italic_B ]
=ЁЭФ╝xтИИXтБв[тИСi=1ksiтБв(x)тБвq^iтБв(x)]absentsubscriptЁЭФ╝ЁЭСеЁЭСЛdelimited-[]superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦ1ЁЭСШsubscriptЁЭСаЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСе\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}_{x\in X}\left[\sum_{i=1}^{k}s_{i}(x)\hat{q}_{i}(x)\right]= blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИИ italic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ]

Thus, sЁЭСаsitalic_s achieves the optimal quality.

тИО

Appendix B Cascading

To prove Theorem┬а2, we heavily rely on the results derived in App.┬аA. As explained in Section┬а3, cascading can be reinterpreted as a sequence of routing problems. However, to prove optimality, we need to be slightly more careful with the exact formulation of the problem.

At step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j, the cascading strategy needs to decide whether to stop the cascade or to continue to the next model. It should continue to the next model if any of the supermodels M1:j,тАж,M1:ksubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧтАжsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСШM_{1:j},\dots,M_{1:k}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is better to run than M1:jтИТ1subscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1M_{1:j-1}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for some measure of тАЩbetterтАЩ. Therefore, the cascading strategy is indeed performing a routing operation between the supermodels M1:jтИТ1,тАж,M1:ksubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1тАжsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСШM_{1:j-1},\dots,M_{1:k}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT.

However, the optimization problem does slightly change compared to the routing problem. First of all, for each query xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X, there is a possibility that the cascade is stopped before step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j. Therefore, the cascade should not aim to optimize the quality at step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j for such a query, since it would not have any effect on the overall quality of the cascade. Furthermore, the budget BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B is only enforced over the entire cascade, and not over the individual steps. Since the problem changes through steps, it is not required that the cost of the router at step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j is exactly equal to BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B.

Therefore, we reformulate cascading using an inner and outer optimization problem. The inner optimization problem aims to find the optimal routing strategy at step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j for a given budget BjsubscriptЁЭР╡ЁЭСЧB_{j}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. The outer optimization problem aims to find the optimal budget BjsubscriptЁЭР╡ЁЭСЧB_{j}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for each step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j such that the overall quality of the cascade is maximized under the constraint that the total cost of the cascade is at most BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B.

To formulate this more exactly, let PjтБв(M)subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧЁЭСАP_{j}(M)italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M ) be the probability that the cascade computed supermodel MЁЭСАMitalic_M by step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j. Then, the inner optimization problem at step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j can be formulated as:

maxr(j)subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСЯЁЭСЧ\displaystyle\max_{r^{(j)}}roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_r start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ЁЭФ╝xтИ╝ЁЭТ│тБв[PjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)тБвтИСi=jтИТ1kr1:iтБв(x)тБвq^1:i(j)тБв(x)]subscriptЁЭФ╝similar-toЁЭСеЁЭТ│delimited-[]subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1ЁЭСШsubscriptЁЭСЯ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСе\displaystyle\mathbb{E}_{x\sim\mathcal{X}}\left[P_{j}(M_{1:j-1})\sum_{i=j-1}^{% k}r_{1:i}(x)\hat{q}^{(j)}_{1:i}(x)\right]blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИ╝ caligraphic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_r start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ] (4)
s.t. ЁЭФ╝xтИ╝ЁЭТ│тБв[PjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)тБвтИСi=jтИТ1kr1:iтБв(x)тБвc^1:i(j)тБв(x)]тй╜BjsubscriptЁЭФ╝similar-toЁЭСеЁЭТ│delimited-[]subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1ЁЭСШsubscriptЁЭСЯ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭР╡ЁЭСЧ\displaystyle\mathbb{E}_{x\sim\mathcal{X}}\left[P_{j}(M_{1:j-1})\sum_{i=j-1}^{% k}r_{1:i}(x)\hat{c}^{(j)}_{1:i}(x)\right]\leqslant B_{j}blackboard_E start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x тИ╝ caligraphic_X end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_r start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ] тй╜ italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT
тИАiтИИ{jтИТ1,тАж,k}:тИАxтИИЁЭТ│:r1:iтБв(x)тЙе0тИзтИСi=jтИТ1kr1:iтБв(x)=1:for-allЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1тАжЁЭСШfor-allЁЭСеЁЭТ│:subscriptЁЭСЯ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСе0superscriptsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1ЁЭСШsubscriptЁЭСЯ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСе1\displaystyle\forall i\in\{j-1,...,k\}:\forall x\in\mathcal{X}:r_{1:i}(x)\geq 0% \land\sum_{i=j-1}^{k}r_{1:i}(x)=1тИА italic_i тИИ { italic_j - 1 , тАж , italic_k } : тИА italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X : italic_r start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) тЙе 0 тИз тИС start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_r start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) = 1

Note that PjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1P_{j}(M_{1:j-1})italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) can be incorporated in the quality and cost estimates. This leaves us with the exact same optimization problem as the routing problem, but with a different budget BjsubscriptЁЭР╡ЁЭСЧB_{j}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since the chosen model only depends on the maximization of PjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)тБвq^i(j)тБв(x)тИТ╬╗jтБвPjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)тБвc^i(j)тБв(x)subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСеP_{j}(M_{1:j-1})\hat{q}^{(j)}_{i}(x)-\lambda_{j}P_{j}(M_{1:j-1})\hat{c}^{(j)}_% {i}(x)italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ), the probability PjтБв(M1:jтИТ1)subscriptЁЭСГЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСА:1ЁЭСЧ1P_{j}(M_{1:j-1})italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) can be divided out of the optimization problem.

The inner optimization problems prove the existence of optimal routing strategies at each step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j with parameters ╬╗jsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧ\lambda_{j}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. We note that there only needs to be one parameter ╬│ЁЭЫ╛\gammaitalic_╬│ that determines the convex combination since the budget BЁЭР╡Bitalic_B is only enforced over the entire cascade.

Let us denote the quality and cost of the entire cascading strategy for given parameters ╬╗1,тАж,╬╗ksubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШ\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and ╬│ЁЭЫ╛\gammaitalic_╬│ as QтБв(╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│)ЁЭСДsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛Q(\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma)italic_Q ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ ) and CтБв(╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│)ЁЭР╢subscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛C(\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma)italic_C ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ ) respectively. Then, the outer optimization problem can be formulated as:

max╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│subscriptsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛\displaystyle\max_{\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma}roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT QтБв(╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│)ЁЭСДsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛\displaystyle Q(\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma)italic_Q ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ ) (5)
s.t. CтБв(╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│)тй╜BЁЭР╢subscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛ЁЭР╡\displaystyle C(\lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma)\leqslant Bitalic_C ( italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ ) тй╜ italic_B

To solve this outer optimization problem, we simply perform a hyperparameter search over the budgets B1,тАж,BksubscriptЁЭР╡1тАжsubscriptЁЭР╡ЁЭСШB_{1},\dots,B_{k}italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_B start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT using a hyperparameter optimization search as discussed in Section┬а3.

B.1 Prior Approximations

We now prove Corollary┬а1. Before doing so, we first need to define what we exactly mean by equivalency. For this purpose, let ЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ1\mathcal{C}_{1}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be defined as follows:

ЁЭТЮ1={sтИгsтБв┬аis a cascading strategy with parameters┬атБв╬╗1,тАж,╬╗k,╬│=0тБв┬аusing estimates┬атБвq^(j),c^(j)}subscriptЁЭТЮ1conditional-setЁЭСаformulae-sequenceЁЭСа┬аis a cascading strategy with parameters┬аsubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШЁЭЫ╛0┬аusing estimates┬аsuperscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧsuperscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧ\mathcal{C}_{1}=\left\{s\mid s\text{ is a cascading strategy with parameters }% \lambda_{1},\dots,\lambda_{k},\gamma=0\text{ using estimates }\hat{q}^{(j)},% \hat{c}^{(j)}\right\}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_s тИг italic_s is a cascading strategy with parameters italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_╬│ = 0 using estimates over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT , over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT }

Similarly, let ЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be defined as follows:

ЁЭТЮ2={sтИгsтБв┬аis a thresholding strategy with parameters┬атБв╧Д1,тАж,╧ДkтБв┬аusing estimates┬атБвq^(j),c^(j)}subscriptЁЭТЮ2conditional-setЁЭСаЁЭСа┬аis a thresholding strategy with parameters┬аsubscriptЁЭЬП1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬПЁЭСШ┬аusing estimates┬аsuperscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЧsuperscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЧ\mathcal{C}_{2}=\left\{s\mid s\text{ is a thresholding strategy with % parameters }\tau_{1},\dots,\tau_{k}\text{ using estimates }\hat{q}^{(j)},\hat{% c}^{(j)}\right\}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_s тИг italic_s is a thresholding strategy with parameters italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT using estimates over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT , over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT }

We note that we set ╬│=0ЁЭЫ╛0\gamma=0italic_╬│ = 0 since the thresholding strategy is deterministic. We therefore restrict the cascading strategy to be deterministic as well.

We define the equivalence between the two sets as follows:

Definition 6 (Equivalence of Strategies).

We say a set of strategies ЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ1\mathcal{C}_{1}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is equivalent to another set of strategies ЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, denoted as ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, if for all s0тИИЁЭТЮ1тИкЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭСа0subscriptЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ2s_{0}\in\mathcal{C}_{1}\cup\mathcal{C}_{2}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИк caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT there exists a s1тИИЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭСа1subscriptЁЭТЮ1s_{1}\in\mathcal{C}_{1}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and a s2тИИЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭСа2subscriptЁЭТЮ2s_{2}\in\mathcal{C}_{2}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT such that for all xтИИЁЭТ│ЁЭСеЁЭТ│x\in\mathcal{X}italic_x тИИ caligraphic_X, s0subscriptЁЭСа0s_{0}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, s1subscriptЁЭСа1s_{1}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and s2subscriptЁЭСа2s_{2}italic_s start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT take the same decisions on xЁЭСеxitalic_x.

We can now more accurately state the conditions under which the thresholding strategy is equivalent to the optimal strategy.

Corollary 2 (Optimal Thresholding Strategy).

Let ЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ1\mathcal{C}_{1}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, ЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be defined as above. Then, ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT if and only if there exists alternative quality and cost estimates q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) and c^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе\hat{c}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) with associated set of cascading strategies ЁЭТЮ1тА▓superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT such that ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ1тА▓subscriptЁЭТЮ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and the following conditions hold on these alternative quality and cost estimates: c^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСРsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСЦЁЭСе\hat{c}^{(j)^{\prime}}_{i}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) is independent of xЁЭСеxitalic_x and bigger than 00, q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСЮsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСЦЁЭСе\hat{q}^{(j)^{\prime}}_{i}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) is independent of xЁЭСеxitalic_x for all iтй╛jЁЭСЦЁЭСЧi\geqslant jitalic_i тй╛ italic_j, and q^1:i(j)тА▓тБв(x)subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСЮsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСе\hat{q}^{(j)^{\prime}}_{1:i}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) is equal to q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)subscriptsuperscript^ЁЭСЮsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСЦЁЭСе\hat{q}^{(j)^{\prime}}_{i}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ).

The main difference between Corollary┬а2 and Corollary┬а1 is that we impose the possibility of alternative quality and cost estimates. However, this does not really influence equivalency in the intuitive sense. Indeed, one could alternatively phrase the corollary as follows: the thresholding strategy is equivalent to any of our cascading strategies if and only if it is possible to construct alternative estimates such that the conditions hold.

Proof.

We note that the cascade sтИИЁЭТЮ1ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭТЮ1s\in\mathcal{C}_{1}italic_s тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT continues on a sample if the following condition holds:

q^1:jтИТ1(j)тБв(x)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^1:jтИТ1(j)тБв(x)<maxiтИИ{j,тАж,k}тБбq^1:i(j)тБв(x)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^1:i(j)тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЧ1ЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:1ЁЭСЧ1ЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧтАжЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q}_{1:j-1}^{(j)}(x)-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{1:j-1}^{(j)}(x)<\max_{i\in\{j,...% ,k\}}\hat{q}_{1:i}^{(j)}(x)-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{1:i}^{(j)}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_j - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) < roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ { italic_j , тАж , italic_k } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) (6)

If ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ1тА▓subscriptЁЭТЮ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, it is clear that Eq.┬а6 reduces to the thresholding strategy for all strategies in ЁЭТЮ1тА▓superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Indeed, for any sтИИЁЭТЮ1тА▓ЁЭСаsuperscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓s\in\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}italic_s тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, set ╧Дj=maxiтИИ{j,тАж,k}тБбq^1:i(j)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^j:i(j)subscriptЁЭЬПЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧтАжЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:ЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ\tau_{j}=\max_{i\in\{j,...,k\}}\hat{q}_{1:i}^{(j)}-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{j:i}^{(% j)}italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ { italic_j , тАж , italic_k } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and the thresholding strategy is equivalent to sЁЭСаsitalic_s. Alternatively, if sтИИЁЭТЮ2ЁЭСаsubscriptЁЭТЮ2s\in\mathcal{C}_{2}italic_s тИИ caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, suppose maxiтИИ{j,тАж,k}тБбq^1:i(j)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^j:i(j)=q^1:i(j)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^j:i(j)subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧтАжЁЭСШsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:ЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:ЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ\max_{i\in\{j,...,k\}}\hat{q}_{1:i}^{(j)}-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{j:i}^{(j)}=\hat{% q}_{1:i}^{(j)}-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{j:i}^{(j)}roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ { italic_j , тАж , italic_k } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT for some index iЁЭСЦiitalic_i. Then, set ╬╗j=╧Дj/c^j:i(j)тИТq^1:i(j)/c^j:i(j)subscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭЬПЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:ЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСР:ЁЭСЧЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ\lambda_{j}=\tau_{j}/\hat{c}_{j:i}^{(j)}-\hat{q}_{1:i}^{(j)}/\hat{c}_{j:i}^{(j)}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT / over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and the cascading strategy is equivalent to sЁЭСаsitalic_s. Therefore, ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ1тА▓тЙбЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT.

Suppose now that ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ1subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. We construct alternative quality and cost estimates q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) and c^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе\hat{c}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) such that the conditions hold and such that ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ1тА▓subscriptЁЭТЮ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. For this purpose, we define c^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)=1superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе1\hat{c}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)=1over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) = 1 for all i,jтИИ{1,тАж,k}ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1тАжЁЭСШi,j\in\{1,\dots,k\}italic_i , italic_j тИИ { 1 , тАж , italic_k }, q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)=1superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе1\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)=1over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) = 1 for all iтй╛jЁЭСЦЁЭСЧi\geqslant jitalic_i тй╛ italic_j, and q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)=q^i(j)тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСеsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦЁЭСЧЁЭСе\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)=\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) = over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) otherwise. Furthermore, we set q^1:i(j)тА▓тБв(x)=q^i(j)тА▓тБв(x)superscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮ:1ЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСеsuperscriptsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСЦsuperscriptЁЭСЧтА▓ЁЭСе\hat{q}_{1:i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)=\hat{q}_{i}^{(j)^{\prime}}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 : italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) = over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_j ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_x ) for all i,jтИИ{1,тАж,k}ЁЭСЦЁЭСЧ1тАжЁЭСШi,j\in\{1,\dots,k\}italic_i , italic_j тИИ { 1 , тАж , italic_k }. The equivalence of ЁЭТЮ1тА▓superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and ЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT can now be proven analogously to the previous paragraph. Therefore, ЁЭТЮ1тЙбЁЭТЮ1тА▓тЙбЁЭТЮ2subscriptЁЭТЮ1superscriptsubscriptЁЭТЮ1тА▓subscriptЁЭТЮ2\mathcal{C}_{1}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{1}^{\prime}\equiv\mathcal{C}_{2}caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тЙб caligraphic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. тИО

Appendix C Cascade Routing

We first note that the proof of the optimality of the cascade routing strategy is equivalent to the proof of the optimality of the cascade strategy, except that the expectation in the optimization problem Eq.┬а4 is now not only over xтИИXЁЭСеЁЭСЛx\in Xitalic_x тИИ italic_X, but also over all possible supermodels that were computed by step jтИТ1ЁЭСЧ1j-1italic_j - 1. However, this does not change the optimization problem, and the proof is completely analogous to the proof given in Section┬а3. Thus, all we need to prove is Lemma┬а1. To prove the lemma, we first prove the following lemma.

Lemma 6.

Let Q1,тАж,QksubscriptЁЭСД1тАжsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСШQ_{1},...,Q_{k}italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be distributions. Let ЁЭТоЁЭТо\mathcal{S}caligraphic_S be the superset of {1,тАж,k}1тАжЁЭСШ\{1,...,k\}{ 1 , тАж , italic_k }. Then f:ЁЭТотЖТтДЭ:ЁЭСУтЖТЁЭТотДЭf:\mathcal{S}\rightarrow\mathbb{R}italic_f : caligraphic_S тЖТ blackboard_R defined as fтБв(S)=ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxiтИИSтБбQi)ЁЭСУЁЭСЖЁЭФ╝subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦf(S)=\mathbb{E}(\max_{i\in S}Q_{i})italic_f ( italic_S ) = blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_S end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) is submodular. Here, we define maxiтИИтИЕтБбQi=тИТтИЮsubscriptЁЭСЦsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\max_{i\in\emptyset}Q_{i}=-\inftyroman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ тИЕ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = - тИЮ

Proof.

Let TтКВSтКВ{1,тАж,k}ЁЭСЗЁЭСЖ1тАжЁЭСШT\subset S\subset\{1,\dots,k\}italic_T тКВ italic_S тКВ { 1 , тАж , italic_k } and jтИИ{1,тАж,k}ЁЭСЧ1тАжЁЭСШj\in\{1,\dots,k\}italic_j тИИ { 1 , тАж , italic_k } be arbitrary. To show the submodularity of fЁЭСУfitalic_f, we need to show that

fтБв(TтИк{j})тИТfтБв(T)тЙеfтБв(SтИк{j})тИТfтБв(S).ЁЭСУЁЭСЗЁЭСЧЁЭСУЁЭСЗЁЭСУЁЭСЖЁЭСЧЁЭСУЁЭСЖf(T\cup\{j\})-f(T)\geq f(S\cup\{j\})-f(S).italic_f ( italic_T тИк { italic_j } ) - italic_f ( italic_T ) тЙе italic_f ( italic_S тИк { italic_j } ) - italic_f ( italic_S ) .

We can write:

fтБв(SтИк{j})тИТfтБв(S)ЁЭСУЁЭСЖЁЭСЧЁЭСУЁЭСЖ\displaystyle f(S\cup\{j\})-f(S)italic_f ( italic_S тИк { italic_j } ) - italic_f ( italic_S ) =ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxiтИИSтИк{j}тБбQi)тИТЁЭФ╝тБв(maxiтИИSтБбQi)absentЁЭФ╝subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЖЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦЁЭФ╝subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}(\max_{i\in S\cup\{j\}}Q_{i})-\mathbb{E}(\max_{i\in S}% Q_{i})= blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_S тИк { italic_j } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) - blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_S end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT )
=ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxтБб(0,QjтИТmaxiтИИSтБбQi))absentЁЭФ╝0subscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЖsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}(\max(0,Q_{j}-\max_{i\in S}Q_{i}))= blackboard_E ( roman_max ( 0 , italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_S end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) )
тй╜ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxтБб(0,QjтИТmaxiтИИTтБбQi))absentЁЭФ╝0subscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЗsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\displaystyle\leqslant\mathbb{E}(\max(0,Q_{j}-\max_{i\in T}Q_{i}))тй╜ blackboard_E ( roman_max ( 0 , italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_T end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) )
=ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxiтИИTтИк{j}тБбQi)тИТЁЭФ╝тБв(maxiтИИTтБбQi)absentЁЭФ╝subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЗЁЭСЧsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦЁЭФ╝subscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЗsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\displaystyle=\mathbb{E}(\max_{i\in T\cup\{j\}}Q_{i})-\mathbb{E}(\max_{i\in T}% Q_{i})= blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_T тИк { italic_j } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) - blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ italic_T end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT )
=fтБв(TтИк{j})тИТfтБв(T).absentЁЭСУЁЭСЗЁЭСЧЁЭСУЁЭСЗ\displaystyle=f(T\cup\{j\})-f(T).= italic_f ( italic_T тИк { italic_j } ) - italic_f ( italic_T ) .

In the proof, we needed maxiтИИтИЕтБбQi=тИТтИЮsubscriptЁЭСЦsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\max_{i\in\emptyset}Q_{i}=-\inftyroman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ тИЕ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = - тИЮ in the case T=тИЕЁЭСЗT=\emptysetitalic_T = тИЕ. тИО

We note that the assertion that maxiтИИтИЕтБбQi=тИТтИЮsubscriptЁЭСЦsubscriptЁЭСДЁЭСЦ\max_{i\in\emptyset}Q_{i}=-\inftyroman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i тИИ тИЕ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_Q start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = - тИЮ corresponds to the fact that giving no answer to a query has тИТтИЮ-\infty- тИЮ quality.

We can now prove Lemma┬а1.

Proof.

Let MЁЭСАMitalic_M and mЁЭСЪmitalic_m be as in the lemma. Suppose MтА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓M^{\prime}italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a supermodel that contains all models in MЁЭСАMitalic_M. Furthermore, let MтА▓тА▓=MтА▓тИЦmsuperscriptЁЭСАтА▓тА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓ЁЭСЪM^{\prime\prime}=M^{\prime}\setminus mitalic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИЦ italic_m. We show that the supermodel MтА▓тА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓тА▓M^{\prime\prime}italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is always strictly preferred over MтА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓M^{\prime}italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. To see this, we note that the difference between ╧ДMтА▓тБв(x,╬╗)subscriptЁЭЬПsuperscriptЁЭСАтА▓ЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ\tau_{M^{\prime}}(x,\lambda)italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x , italic_╬╗ ) and ╧ДMтА▓тА▓тБв(x,╬╗)subscriptЁЭЬПsuperscriptЁЭСАтА▓тА▓ЁЭСеЁЭЬЖ\tau_{M^{\prime\prime}}(x,\lambda)italic_╧Д start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x , italic_╬╗ ) is equal to

ЁЭФ╝тБв(maxmтА▓тИИMтА▓тБбq^mтА▓тБв(x))тИТЁЭФ╝тБв(maxmтА▓тИИMтА▓тА▓тБбq^mтА▓тБв(x))тИТ╬╗jтБвc^mтБв(x)ЁЭФ╝subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСЪтА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓subscript^ЁЭСЮsuperscriptЁЭСЪтА▓ЁЭСеЁЭФ╝subscriptsuperscriptЁЭСЪтА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓тА▓subscript^ЁЭСЮsuperscriptЁЭСЪтА▓ЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЪЁЭСе\mathbb{E}(\max_{m^{\prime}\in M^{\prime}}\hat{q}_{m^{\prime}}(x))-\mathbb{E}(% \max_{m^{\prime}\in M^{\prime\prime}}\hat{q}_{m^{\prime}}(x))-\lambda_{j}\hat{% c}_{m}(x)blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ) - blackboard_E ( roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тИИ italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x )

By Lemma┬а6, this difference is smaller than q^MтБв(x)тИТq^MтИЦ{m}тБв(x)тИТ╬╗jтБвc^mтБв(x)subscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСАЁЭСеsubscript^ЁЭСЮЁЭСАЁЭСЪЁЭСеsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСЧsubscript^ЁЭСРЁЭСЪЁЭСе\hat{q}_{M}(x)-\hat{q}_{M\setminus\{m\}}(x)-\lambda_{j}\hat{c}_{m}(x)over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - over^ start_ARG italic_q end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M тИЦ { italic_m } end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ) - italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT over^ start_ARG italic_c end_ARG start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_m end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_x ). Thus, by assumption, this difference is negative, and therefore MтА▓тА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓тА▓M^{\prime\prime}italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is always preferred over MтА▓superscriptЁЭСАтА▓M^{\prime}italic_M start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT тА▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, which concludes the proof. тИО

Appendix D Experimental Details

We describe some additional details about the experimental setup and the datasets used in our experiments.

D.1 Routerbench

Data Split

We use 5%percent55\%5 % of the RouterBench data (around 2000200020002000 samples) to optimize the hyperparameters of cascading, routing, and cascade routing. The remaining 95%percent9595\%95 % is used for evaluation. We use the same data split for all noise levels.

Noise

In Table┬а3 we specify the standard deviations of the noise levels on the RouterBench dataset. To put these numbers into context, we note that quality varies between 00 and 1111, and the average cost of the smallest models is 0.0000730.0000730.0000730.000073, while the average cost of the largest models is 0.0032810.0032810.0032810.003281. We fit a logistic regression model on this noisy signal to obtain the quality and cost estimates. This simulates the noise in the features that are used to estimate the quality and cost of the models.

Models

In the evaluated scenarios for three models, we use the models Mixtral-8x7b-Chat, GPT-3.5-Turbo-1106, and GPT-4-1106-Preview. When using five models, we add WizardLM-13B-V1.2 and Claude-v2 to the mix. For eleven models, we use all models available in the benchmark.

D.2 Accurate Quality Estimation

Data Split

For the SWE-Bench benchmark, we use its verified data split and divide the dataset into training and calibration subsets, with each comprising 50%percent5050\%50 % of the data. For the Minerva Math and LiveCodeBench benchmark, we only include the Algebra portion of Minerva Math to ensure that both benchmarks have a comparable number of samples for evaluation. Similarly, we also perform a 50%percent5050\%50 % split of this dataset into training and calibration sets.

Evaluation Setting

For the SWE-Bench evaluation, we analyze the performance of 10 models submitted to the benchmarkтАЩs leaderboard. The logs for these models were obtained from the official SWE-Bench repository222https://github.com/swe-bench/experiments. Specifically, we evaluated the following models:

  • тАв

    20240402_sweagent_claude3opus

  • тАв

    20241007_nfactorial

  • тАв

    20240728_sweagent_gpt4o

  • тАв

    20240620_sweagent_claude3.5sonnet

  • тАв

    20241016_epam-ai-run-gpt-4o

  • тАв

    20240824_gru

  • тАв

    20241106_navie-2-gpt4o-sonnet

  • тАв

    20240820_epam-ai-run-gpt-4o

  • тАв

    20241202_agentless-1.5_claude-3.5-sonnet-20241022

  • тАв

    20241028_agentless-1.5_gpt4o

For each model, we extract the time required to complete a task to measure cost.

For LiveCodeBench and Minerva Math, we evaluate the following models:

  • тАв

    Qwen-2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct

  • тАв

    Qwen-2.5-Coder-1.5B-Instruct

  • тАв

    Qwen-2.5-Math-7B-Instruct

  • тАв

    Qwen-2.5-Math-1.5B-Instruct

We conduct experiments using version 5 of the LiveCodeBench benchmark from its official repository. For Minerva Math, we utilize the LM Evaluation Harness (Gao et┬аal., 2024) to ensure consistent and reliable evaluation.

Cost Estimation

For SWE-Bench, the cost is defined as the time (in seconds) that a model takes to complete a task. A linear regression model is fitted to predict this cost based on the query length and, when available, the cost of running other models.

For LiveCodeBench and Minerva Math, the cost is calculated as the total number of tokens in both the query and the answer, multiplied by the size of the model (in billions of parameters). Similar to SWE-Bench, a linear model is used to predict the cost based on query length and other modelsтАЩ costs.

Quality Estimation

For ex-ante quality estimation in SWE-Bench, we train a logistic regression model that predicts quality based on the query length and a one-hot encoded variable representing the queryтАЩs source repository. Post-hoc quality estimation leverages the ground-truth quality scores computed during evaluation.

For ex-ante quality estimation in Minerva Math and LiveCodeBench, we include the query length, query source (Minerva Math or LiveCodeBench), and the difficulty level of the problem as defined by the benchmark. Post-hoc quality estimation incorporates additional information, such as whether the parsed answers from different models agree with one another.

D.3 Poor Quality Estimation

Data Split

We split each dataset in each benchmark into a training set and a test set, each comprising 50%percent5050\%50 % of the data. For all datasets except GSM8k, the training set is created by splitting the original test data. In the case of GSM8k, since a separate training set is already available, we use this pre-existing training data, leaving the original test set unchanged. The training set is then further divided, with 50%percent5050\%50 % used for training quality and cost estimators, and the remaining 50%percent5050\%50 % reserved for hyperparameter optimization through validation.

Evaluation Setting

We use completion-based evaluation in a one-shot setting for each benchmark. For the classification tasks, we obtain the probability associated with each class ("A", "B", "C", тАж) from the model directly. For open-form reasoning tasks, we extract the answer by instruction the model to generate a completion that ends with an extractable answer. If the model does not output an answer in the correct format, we perform a best-effort extraction by trying various regex patterns. Details on the prompts and regex patterns used for each benchmark are provided in the code repository.

Models

For the Llama-3.1 model family, we use the models Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct, and Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct. For the Gemma model family, we use the models Gemma-2B-Instruct, Gemma-2-9B-Instruct, and Gemma-2-27B-Instruct. For the Mistral model family, we use the models Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3, Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1, and Mixtral-8x22B-Instruct-v0.1.

Cost Estimation

For cost estimation, we first calculate the number of tokens in both the query and the modelтАЩs response. We then use API-based prices per token for each model to estimate the cost.333We used the Together API for all our experiments. In classification, where responses consist of a single token, the cost can be determined before running the model. In open-form reasoning tasks, where response lengths vary, we estimate this length based on responses from previous models in the cascade if the model has not yet been computed. If no model response is available, we estimate the response length using the average from the training data.

Features Quality Estimates

We specify the exact features used for the logistic regression model that serves as the quality estimator in Section┬а5.2. First, we include a one-hot encoding of the various datasets in each benchmark. Furthermore, for classification, we include the probability associated with the highest class and the entropy of the class probabilities if the model has been computed. If several models have been computed, we include both whether they agree on their prediction, and the JS-divergence between their class probabilities. For open-form reasoning, we include the perplexity, number of tokens, and several quantiles of the logits if the model has been computed, in accordance with Gupta et┬аal. (2024). If several models have been computed, we also include whether they agree on their prediction.

We note that we train a separate logistic regression model for each history of computed models, and for each model separately as well. Thus we have one linear model for each combination of a target model misubscriptЁЭСЪЁЭСЦm_{i}italic_m start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and computed models mi1,тАж,mijsubscriptЁЭСЪsubscriptЁЭСЦ1тАжsubscriptЁЭСЪsubscriptЁЭСЦЁЭСЧm_{i_{1}},\dots,m_{i_{j}}italic_m start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_m start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. All the linear models are trained on the training set included in the benchmark.

Appendix E Confidence Intervals

To check whether the results obtained by cascade routing are significantly higher than our baselines in Tables┬а1, 2 and┬а10, we perform bootstrapping on the samples in the dataset. Specifically, we compute the confidence interval associated with the difference between the AUC scores of cascade routing and the baselines. If this difference is positive and its 2тБв╧Г2ЁЭЬО2\sigma2 italic_╧Г confidence interval does not contain zero, we can conclude that cascade routing is significantly better than the baseline. These confidence intervals are reported in Tables┬а4, 5 and┬а6.

Appendix F Additional Experiments

F.1 Ablation Study

We conduct an ablation study to examine the impact of various design choices in cascade routing on performance and runtime. Runtime is a critical factor because the overhead introduced by the strategy must be negligible compared to the time required for model computation. If the strategy adds significant overhead, its performance gains may be offset by the increased runtime. We also include an additional ablation that specifically targets runtime on random data in Section┬аF.2.

To investigate this, we repeat the experiment from Section┬а5.1 when using all eleven models, testing different variations of cascade routing. We evaluate a slower variation that omits Lemma┬а1, thereby requiring more supermodels to be evaluated (Slow), a greedy variation that only considers supermodels of length j+1ЁЭСЧ1j+1italic_j + 1 at step jЁЭСЧjitalic_j (Greedy), and a version that does not compute the expected value when evaluating supermodel quality, using the quality of the best model instead (No-Expect).

Results

Table┬а7 presents the results. As expected, the Slow variation is almost an order of magnitude slower while achieving similar performance. In contrast, both Greedy and No-Expect are faster but perform worse in the low- and medium-noise scenarios by 0.5%percent0.50.5\%0.5 % to 1.3%percent1.31.3\%1.3 %. Interestingly, there is a much smaller performance gap in the high-noise scenario. This is due to the very low variance in the quality estimates, since the linear model used for quality estimation predicts an almost constant value for each query in this scenario, making the expected value computation less important.

Furthermore, the Greedy and No-Expect variants perform very similarly, while Greedy is about twice as fast as No-Expect. This suggests that one should almost always use the normal variant of cascade routing, and only consider the Greedy variant if runtime is a critical concern. Neither the Slow nor the No-Expect variant is recommended, as they either perform worse or are significantly slower than the normal variant.

F.2 Runtime Analysis

We further analyze the runtime of the four variants of cascade routing presented in Section┬аF.1. Specifically, we perform experiments with random data, scaling the number of models to 80 to evaluate the runtime of all variants. Furthermore, we include a fifth variant of cascade routing in the analysis Max-Depth, which restricts cascade routing to a maximum depth of 3333 models. Max-Depth does not reduce performance of cascade routing if the optimal depth is less than or equal to 3333 models. However, it does significantly reduce the runtime of cascade routing.

For each number of models, we generate 100100100100 data points, each with random quality and cost estimates associated with each model. For each point, we generate the hyperparameters ╬╗1,тАж,╬╗ksubscriptЁЭЬЖ1тАжsubscriptЁЭЬЖЁЭСШ\lambda_{1},...,\lambda_{k}italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , тАж , italic_╬╗ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and ╬│ЁЭЫ╛\gammaitalic_╬│ randomly. We then report the average runtime of the five variants of cascade routing in Fig.┬а3.

The results show the varying computational complexity of the different variants of cascade routing. Slow has the highest runtime, and becomes computationally too expensive even when using less than 20202020 models. In contrast, standard cascade routing has a significantly lower runtime, and is able to handle up to 40404040 models within a 1111 second runtime. Its faster variant, Max-Depth, is able to handle up to 80808080 models within a 1111 second runtime. Furthermore, we now also see a clear difference between No-Expect and Greedy. While Greedy remains computationally very cheap even for 80808080 models, No-Expect has a significantly higher runtime, even obtaining higher runtimes than Max-Depth for 80808080 models.

Thus, the conclusions from Section┬аF.1 are further supported by the runtime analysis: Greedy is the most efficient variant of cascade routing, while Normal is the most efficient variant that does not compromise performance. Max-Depth is a good choice if the optimal depth is known to be less than or equal to 3333 models, as it significantly reduces runtime without compromising performance. Since cascades of more than 3333 models are rare, Max-Depth is a good choice in practice.

Appendix G Detailed Results

We present benchmark-specific AUC values for the experiment performed in Section┬а5.2 in Table┬а8 for classification and Table┬а9 for open-form reasoning. In Fig.┬а4, we show the quality-cost tradeoff curves for several benchmarks. The curves are obtained by varying the cost threshold ╬╗ЁЭЬЖ\lambdaitalic_╬╗ and plotting the resulting accuracy and cost values in a curve.

Appendix H Additional Results

In Table┬а10 we report the AUC scores for the RouterBench dataset for different noise levels for the five-shot evaluation. Our conclusions presented in Section┬а5.1 remain consistent with the results presented in Table┬а10. However, there is one notable inconsistency: in two of the three low-noise scenarios, our cascading strategy performs worse than the threshold-based baseline cascade. In the scenario with three models, we find its cause can be found in the more difficult optimization surface for the hyperparameters of our cascading strategy. Specifically, our cascading strategy at some point starts to lose quality as cost increases. By simply setting the hyperparameters of the cascading strategy once it starts to lose quality to the ones where it obtained its highest quality, we obtain a quality of 83.35%percent83.3583.35\%83.35 % over the 83.17%percent83.1783.17\%83.17 % of the baseline cascade.

In contrast, for low-noise and eleven models, a similar approach does not yield a better result. Rather, the discrepancy is caused by a small mismatch between the quality estimates of supermodels and the chosen model. While the quality estimate is based on the expected maximum of all models, we restrict the selected model to be the last model that was computed in the cascade. Since the expected maximum is higher than the quality of the last model, this discrepancy can lead to suboptimal decisions. By allowing both the baseline cascade and our cascading strategy to select the model with the highest quality estimate, we find that our cascading strategy once again outperforms the baseline cascade. Note that this slight discrepancy is not relevant for cascade routing, since the extra restriction is not imposed in this setting.
